title: fix: nunchaku CUDA hang prevention + Sana 1.6B Lambda infra docs
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Un-hanging the GPUs
gist.blurb: We taught our image servers how to not freeze up when they get overwhelmed, and swapped in Sana Sprint 1.6B while we were under the hood. Less hanging, more generating.
gist.summary: Fixed CUDA hang issues in the nunchaku image server and updated our infrastructure to replace SDXL Turbo with Sana Sprint 1.6B on Lambda workers.
gist.impact: Users will experience significantly more reliable image generation with fewer timeouts, powered by the new Sana Sprint 1.6B model.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a mechanic fixing a server rack to prevent 'hangs', with the Monitor Robot showing a healthy status screen. Represents fixing the CUDA OOM/hangs and upgrading the GPU workers.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny mechanic's cap, using a wrench to fix a retro server rack that glows with a healthy lime green (#ecf874) light. The chunky Monitor Robot stands nearby giving a happy thumbs up, its screen showing a smooth running graph. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
